senses.definition: A hypothetical or provisional subject before a main subject in a discussion or document.
examples: The treaty draft begins with a presubject outlining the context of the negotiations.
In the syllabus, there is a presubject that explains the course goals before the main topics.

senses.definition: A state or condition of being subject to something in advance; pre-subjection.
examples: The presubjection of terms in the contract created early obligations.
Some policies are formed under presubjection to international norms.

senses.definition: The act of submitting something beforehand; pre-submission.
examples: The presubmission review helps editors catch issues early.
Submit the proposal after the presubmission checks are complete.

senses.definition: To submit something beforehand; to present for review before the final submission.
examples: Please presubmit the report for initial feedback.
They presubmit the code to the repository for peer review.

senses.definition: Having been submitted beforehand; submitted prior to the intended final submission.
examples: The presubmitted documents were reviewed by the committee.
Presubmitted proposals can be edited before the final submission.

senses.definition: The act of submitting something beforehand; the process of presubmitting.
examples: Presubmitting the manuscript speeds up the review process.
They are presubmitting the preliminary data for early feedback.

senses.definition: Assigned or placed under a subordinate status before formal subordination; pre-subordinate.
examples: The presubordinate role was temporary until official approval.
A presubordinate clause in the contract specifies preliminary duties.

senses.definition: Placed under subordinate status beforehand; already pre-subordinated.
examples: The presubordinated division reported to the manager.
Presubordinated tasks were reassigned after the restructure.

senses.definition: Acting to place under subordinate status in advance; pre-subordinating.
examples: They are presubordinating certain roles to streamline the chain of command.
Presubordinating tasks helps prepare for the new structure.

senses.definition: The act or state of being subordinated in advance; pre-subordination.
examples: Presubordination arrangements affected the reporting lines.
There was a presubordination plan for the department.

senses.definition: To subscribe or sign up in advance; pre-subscribe.
examples: Users can presubscribe to early-access features.
Presubscribe to the newsletter to receive updates.

senses.definition: Having subscribed in advance; pre-subscribed.
examples: Presubscribed readers received the beta notes.
Presubscribed users got special onboarding materials.

senses.definition: A person who subscribes in advance; a pre-subscriber.
examples: Presubscribers will receive early access codes.
The presubscriber list grew quickly before launch.

senses.definition: The act of subscribing in advance; pre-subscribing.
examples: Presubscribing users get early access emails.
Presubscribing is optional but recommended.

senses.definition: The act or state of subscribing in advance; pre-subscription.
examples: A presubscription period was offered to early adopters.
The presubscription gives access to exclusive content.

senses.definition: To exist or continue in a pre-defined or provisional way.
examples: The plan presubsists until final approval is granted.
Presubsisting measures kept the project running.

senses.definition: A state of existing with minimal resources; provisional subsistence.
examples: The community faced presubsistence during the drought.
Presubsistence strategies were adopted while awaiting aid.

senses.definition: Existing or continuing in a provisional or minimal state.
examples: Presubsistent conditions hinder long-term planning.
The presubsistent economy was unstable.

senses.definition: Before attaining substantial significance or amount; not yet substantial.
examples: Presubstantial evidence was presented, but more is needed.
The presubstantial phase of the project will be followed by a full rollout.

senses.definition: A substitute prepared in advance; a pre-substitute.
examples: The presubstitute was ready in case the primary chef fell ill.
A presubstitute was named to avoid delays.

senses.definition: Substituted in advance; pre-substituted.
examples: The presubstituted item replaced the original briefly.
Presubstituted staff filled in during the meeting.

senses.definition: The act of substituting in advance; pre-substituting.
examples: Presubstituting parts kept the process moving.
They are presubstituting teammates for the next phase.

senses.definition: The act or process of substituting in advance; pre-substitution.
examples: Presubstitution plans were outlined in the contract.
Presubstitution reduces downtime.

senses.definition: A state of expecting success in advance; pre-success.
examples: There is presuccess among the team after months of hard work.
The plan built presuccess by setting early milestones.

senses.definition: Describing something aimed at ensuring future success.
examples: Presuccess strategies were discussed in the meeting.
They implemented presuccess measures to boost morale.

senses.definition: Expected to be successful; likely to succeed.
examples: The presuccessful plan won approval quickly.
Presuccessful outcomes depend on proper preparation.
